title: Iran Risks Peace Talks With U.S. to Maintain Leverage Over Strait
summary: Iran sees its control over the Strait of Hormuz as critical leverage in peace talks with the United States. It seems willing to risk the cease-fire to maintain that power.

title: Here’s What to Know About the Standoff in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The United States and Iran have engaged in rounds of attacks and counterattacks in recent days, eroding hopes for a quick return to normalcy in the Middle East.

title: Trump Cut a Billion-Dollar Mining Deal. His Sons Stand to Profit.
summary: An agreement between the U.S. and Kazakhstan has given a group of American investors with ties to the president and the commerce secretary access to one of the world’s largest untapped reserves of tungsten.

title: Rats, Leaks and Broken Elevators: Repair Backlog Plagues Federal Buildings
summary: After decades, deferred maintenance totals an estimated $50 billion. But getting repair funds from Congress is a laborious process.

title: In Venezuela, a Community Comes Together to Search for Earthquake Survivors
summary: Volunteers in a middle-class neighborhood in Caracas used drills, picks and hammers to break through concrete, trying to find anyone in need of rescue.

title: 12 Hours With Venezuelan Doctors Searching for Earthquake Survivors
summary: A medical team set out from the capital to rescue people in the hardest-hit part of the disaster zone, La Guaira. They found silence in the ruins instead.

title: Three Firefighters Die as Wildfires Ravage Utah and Colorado
summary: Fires are raging in the arid Southwest after a warm winter. Forecasters say the fires may grow rapidly, and parts of Colorado have been ordered to evacuate.

title: A ‘Heat Dome’ Could Bring Triple- Digit Temperatures to the Midwest and Eastern U.S.
summary: Forecasters warned that temperatures could break records in some areas. The heat is expected to last into the Fourth of July holiday weekend.

title: Trump Wants to Own the Nation’s 250th Birthday. States Have Their Own Plans.
summary: As partisan tensions swirl around festivities in Washington, red and blue states alike are leaning into complex history — and their own stories.

title: How Much Have Airfares Risen With High Jet Fuel Costs?
summary: While prices are up overall, there’s a surprising pattern depending on your destination.

title: Pride March Caps Joyous New York June, but Anxiety Tempers Hope
summary: The annual celebration on Sunday took place in a city where many in the L.G.B.T.Q. community are optimistic about their local leaders but face challenges from the Trump administration.

title: Philippe Stern, Heir to the Patek Philippe Watch Brand, Dies at 88
summary: In an era of electronic timepieces, he marketed $40,000 handmade watches as status symbols, breathing new life into a struggling industry.

title: The Hamptons’ Essential Luxury Good: Human Labor
summary: The people who want to visit can’t function without the people who have to live there.

title: If These Celebrities Are Democracy’s Last Hope, We’re in Trouble
summary: When speaking up actually means risking something, for once, it is more necessary than ever.

title: Bill Maher Set to Receive Mark Twain Prize at the Kennedy Center
summary: Mr. Maher, a frequent critic of President Trump, is receiving a top comedy award at the institution that once bore the president’s name.
